plan: Step 1: Run `npm install` to restore node_modules in the worktree (package.json and lockfile already exist from US-001)
Step 2: Verify `npm run lint` executes successfully — eslint.config.js already exists with recommended + typescript-eslint configs and proper ignores
Step 3: Verify `npm run typecheck` (`tsc --noEmit`) passes — tsconfig.json is already configured with strict mode

Step 4: Verify `npm run test` (`vitest run`) passes — vitest.config.ts is configured and existing unit tests exist in tests/unit/ (clampPixelRatio.test.ts, initRenderer.test.ts, shell.test.ts, workspace-setup.test.ts)
Step 5: If any script fails, fix the underlying issue (lint errors, type errors, or test failures) — the configs and scripts themselves are already correctly defined

Step 6: If no sample domain unit test exists yet, add a minimal one in tests/unit/ that tests a pure function (e.g. clampPixelRatio) to satisfy the 'a sample unit test passes' acceptance criterion — though tests/unit/clampPixelRatio.test.ts likely already covers this
Step 7: Run all three scripts in sequence (`npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test`) to confirm they all pass as a final gate
Step 8: Commit the changes (if any were needed beyond npm install)
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run lint` and confirm zero errors/warnings exit code 0
Run `npm run typecheck` and confirm tsc reports no type errors
Run `npm run test` and confirm vitest reports all tests passing with exit code 0
Verify at least one unit test exercises a pure function (e.g. clampPixelRatio) to satisfy the sample unit test criterion
Run the full quality gate: `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` in sequence to confirm all pass together
risks: The worktree lacks node_modules — npm install must succeed before any script can run. If the lockfile is stale or dependencies have conflicts, this could block everything.
The existing workspace-setup.test.ts shells out to npm run build/typecheck/lint inside tests, which makes tests slower and could cause circular failures if the environment isn't fully set up.
ESLint v10 with flat config and typescript-eslint may flag issues in existing code that weren't caught during US-001 if lint wasn't run at that time.
